title: pull/from/commit/push: retry on most failures
body:
If `PullOptions`/`BuilderOptions`/`CommitOptions`/`PushOptions`/`BuildOptions` includes a `MaxRetries` value other than 0, retry operations except for (currently) connection-refused, authentication, and no-such-repository/no-such-tag errors, at a default-but-configurable interval of 5 seconds.

Set the default for `buildah pull/from/commit/push` to 3 retries at 2 second intervals.

comments.body: Sadly this pushed us over the travis time limit.
Do you plan to expose these options?  Should these options be handled in containers.conf?

I hadn't planned to expose the settings (the main thing is that the CLI opts in to it, but the API doesn't do it by default, so that OpenShift doesn't see a change in behavior, since they already have retry logic of their own), but we can if we need to.  Opinions?
I am fine without adding CLI support.

Changes LGTM.  I'm guessing the changes have caused our "expect to fail" test to pause and retry a number of times causing Travis to hit the 50 minute timeout.  Prior we were completing in about 46 to 47 minutes.  The Travis timeout can't be changed unless we go with a paid plan or remove Travis entirely.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 09d1c24) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:-1: Rejected by too few approved reviews
